fact: November 25 is exactly 35 days before the end of the year - that's exactly 5 weeks!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when squirrels go absolutely nuts burying their last acorns before winter really hits!
The sun sets before 5 PM in most of the northern United States around this date - talk about early bedtime vibes!
If you're born on November 25, you're a Sagittarius - the archer who shoots arrows at the stars!
In Antarctica, November 25 means the sun never sets and penguin chicks are getting their first swimming lessons!
This date falls smack in the middle of what meteorologists call 'pre-winter' - when the air gets that sharp, nose-tingling bite!
November 25 is exactly one month before Christmas Day - Santa's elves are probably having panic attacks right about now!
In northern Canada and Alaska, the aurora borealis (northern lights) start getting super active around this time because of longer, darker nights!
Trees in temperate regions have dropped about 90% of their leaves by November 25 - making the world look like it's covered in nature's confetti!
This is when bears in cold climates are getting seriously sleepy and looking for the perfect cave to snooze in for months!
November 25 is roughly 34 days after the autumn equinox - meaning we're deep into the 'getting darker every day' zone in the north!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, November 25 is prime time for baby animals to be born because spring is in full swing!
